storyInfo: Enciodes formally takes control of the Vine-Bead Court and arrests Elder Adso for colluding with Victoria. However, Caster turns the tables, publicly declaring that Karlan Trade is Victoria's greatest ally. Deep within the mines, Elena and her companions are disappointed to discover that their theories about the source of the Catastrophe are completely wrong.

attributes.content: Hurry it up! You in the back, keep up!
attributes.name: Distant Voice

attributes.content: Our intel indicates that the target should be around here. Search carefully, and leave no stone unturned!
attributes.name: Distant Voice

attributes.content: After that woman left the room, an unknown amount of time passed, to the point that time itself seemed alien to Monch.
Only the stinging pain of the ropes tightly binding her reminds her that she has not yet lost consciousness.
Finally, a hopeful sound comes from beyond the door, and Monch musters what might she has left to shout out.
I'm... over here...
attributes.name: Monch

attributes.content: I'm—
attributes.name: Monch

attributes.content: But try as she might, her parched throat can hardly produce a sound.
She laboriously crawls towards the door.
It opens.
Monch!
attributes.name: Sciurus

attributes.content: Madam... Why are you here...?
attributes.name: Monch

attributes.content: We've been searching for you ever since the Catastrophe hit! That damned Silverash, pretentious bastard. Thought they actually had some useful intel... but in the end, I still ended up having to do all the legwork myself to find you!
attributes.name: Sciurus

attributes.content: How're you doing? You hurt anywhere?
attributes.name: Sciurus

attributes.content: Wait, your leg—
attributes.name: Sciurus

attributes.content: I'm... fine...
attributes.name: Monch

attributes.content: Madam, listen to me...
attributes.name: Monch

attributes.content: The researcher I'm tracking... She's from Victoria, but more important... she's got ties, to Columbia...
attributes.name: Monch

attributes.content: What they're really after is...
attributes.name: Monch

attributes.content: Monch! MONCH!
attributes.name: Sciurus

attributes.content: Along the narrow mountain path, the convoy slowly plods onward.
After the incident's sudden outbreak, no shortage of people left their cars to observe the conflict's epicenter.
Turn off the engine. Get out.
attributes.name: Matterhorn

attributes.content: What do you think you're doing? Who the hell are you people?
attributes.name: Victorian Agent

attributes.content: I... I'm an industrial investigator dispatched from Victoria! This is illegal obstruction of official business!
attributes.name: Victorian Agent

attributes.content: You've damage our property, endangered Victorian lives, and almost even caused an accident! Mark my words, the Vine-Bead Court will hear of this!
attributes.name: Victorian Agent

attributes.content: Never should've come to this bloody shithole...
attributes.name: Victorian Agent

attributes.content: ...Are you done?
attributes.name: Matterhorn

attributes.content: Did you get all the pictures?
attributes.name: Matterhorn

attributes.content: Yessir!
attributes.name: Silverash Clan Guard

attributes.content: This is no time for you to try to twist the narrative, Victorian. Just cooperate.
attributes.name: Matterhorn

attributes.content: Open up the rear compartment.
attributes.name: Matterhorn

attributes.content: Unwilling to cooperate? Fine by me. You're more than welcome to conserve your energy to fend for yourselves before the court–if you're cold, I can offer you a blanket.
attributes.name: Matterhorn

attributes.content: Matterhorn waves his hand, and the guard pries open the rear compartment without a word.
Seated upright upon a haystack there is an old cleric.
He brushes the dust off his body and walks out.
It is an emotionless affair, hardly amounting to courage.
The old cleric gazes upon the blinding brightness before him, and recollects the scenes from before.
Some ten minutes ago...
Why'd the truck stop?
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: Have we arrived?
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: Carolin lowers her voice.
...Did something happen?
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: Stay calm, young lady.
attributes.name: Elderly Cleric

attributes.content: ...Did they find us?
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: I didn't expect to find you here, Cleric of the Vine-Bead Court.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Let's continue this conversation elsewhere. This place is less than ideal.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: You are as hasty as always, young master of Clan Edelweiss.
attributes.name: Elderly Cleric

attributes.content: Honorable friends of Clan Silverash, I apologize for disturbing your work during this inclement weather.
attributes.name: Elderly Cleric

attributes.content: I am more than glad to go along with your arrangements.
attributes.name: Elderly Cleric

attributes.content: Looks like the one we're after isn't here.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: I do believe this is where we part ways, young lady from Victoria.
attributes.name: Elderly Cleric

attributes.content: Are you getting off?
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: No, I'll stay here. Just keep walking forward, and don't look back.
attributes.name: Elderly Cleric

attributes.content: The entrance isn't far off. Silverash's people will surely notice this truck. Get out of here before they find you.
attributes.name: Elderly Cleric

attributes.content: By Kjeragandr... trust me.
attributes.name: Elderly Cleric

attributes.content: The old cleric helps Carolin up.
I'll do as you said.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: But, you'll also be exposed.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: That, too, is Kjeragandr's will. What I cannot change, I shall therefore accept.
attributes.name: Elderly Cleric

attributes.content: I ask only for Her forgiveness.
attributes.name: Elderly Cleric

attributes.content: I can't say I understand what your beliefs truly are—but maybe some day, I will.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: I respect your beliefs, so I will also try to find a way to live in peace. Best of luck to you, sir.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: Carolin gets out of the truck and runs without stopping, in a direction leading far, far from Kjerag.
Shedding her stained cleric's robes, she shivers from the cold.
Alone she walks through the vast, pale mountainscape.
Until in the distance, a dark tunnel entrance beckons to her.
Through the telescope's lens, Gnosis sees the mysterious scientist step into the shadows.
Arrest these two and have them sent straight to the Vine-Bead Court.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: I've brought one.
attributes.name: Degenbrecher

attributes.content: I'm afraid I cannot grant your wish, Great Elder.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Upon hearing that long-awaited voice, Enciodes stands up and walks past the old man who remains frozen like a wooden sculpture.
Gnosis executed the plan. The Tschäggättä deployed immediately on his signal.
attributes.name: Degenbrecher

attributes.content: Before the presence of these wraiths, the clerics could do nothing but stumble back into their abodes, their tender faces affixed in fear.
I, I'm one of the Vine-Bead Court's clerics! You can't treat me like this—
attributes.name: Vine-Bead Apprentice Cleric

attributes.content: Shut up and stop resisting!
attributes.name: Tschäggättä

attributes.content: ...Master Frey is here too! Please, help me!
attributes.name: Vine-Bead Apprentice Cleric

attributes.content: The one just brought in was the once-arrogant envoy of the Vine-Bead Court. His gaze sweeps over the shouting cleric, and he shakes his head.
Father Frey, I believe I can now explain your actions.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Victoria's agents conspired with the Vine-Bead Court's clerics to smuggle spies out of the country through the mine railway, but were caught red-handed thanks to Karlan Trade.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: And before that, you were assisting the Victorians with scouring the mining site. Premeditated ill-intent.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: And lest you forget, you protected the suspect following Ratatos's funeral.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: You will formally be put on trial for these crimes.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: By Kjeragandr... Forgive him, for he knows not of what he speaks––
attributes.name: Vine-Bead Court Envoy

attributes.content: It is a fact that spies have infiltrated the Vine-Bead Court. I urge you to not resist. Cooperation with our investigation is the only path to leniency.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Take Elder Great Adso, Father Frey, and the other elders of the Vine-Bead Court away for questioning. The other clerics are to remain there, awaiting their summons.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Yessir!
attributes.name: Tschäggättä

attributes.content: Now then, Great Elder Adso––
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Silverash, do you think you've won?
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: 'Those who live by the blade will find their arrogance short-lived.'
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: I wonder, just how long will your arbitrary reign of terror last?
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Without making any unnecessary movements, Adso gives a slight nod to the crowd, then slowly walks to the door.
Great Elder! What should we do?!
attributes.name: Vine-Bead Apprentice Cleric

attributes.content: ...Survive.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: The rest is already written in the scriptures. I have nothing else to say.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: You are Her servants, and whatever happens, you must act according to your conscience.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Take him away.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: By Kjeragandr, may the guilty receive their fair trial.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: This is an abandoned mining shaft—the torches and tunnel lights don't work anymore. Bend down carefully, and watch your head...
attributes.name: Joanna
Joanna
attributes.content: If we wrap around from this side, we should just be able to make it...

attributes.name: Joanna
attributes.content: Urgh!
Watch out!
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: ...Come over first. Watch your step.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Joanna shifts her feet, and several rocks tumble down the cave, the sounds of dislodged pebbles echoing through the cracked crevices as they fall. She shudders.
So deep... You'd probably turn into a stain on the ground if you fell from this height...
attributes.name: Joanna
Joanna
attributes.content: Damn those Victorians for causing trouble in the mines... Else, we wouldn't have to come this way...

attributes.name: Joanna
attributes.content: Huh? Saintess, what are you looking at?
There's a light ahead; it must be the path leading to the main ore vein. Come—let's hold hands, just like before.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: We are just a few steps shy of our answer. We mustn't run into danger here.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Soon... We will find all our answers.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The map is illuminated by the flickering light of the oil lamp. They hardly converse, moving forward in silence, their steps filled with both fear and anticipation.
But eventually, the search comes to an end.
...Are you sure this is the right place? Might we have made a mistake?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: No... I'm sure of it.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: This is the mine I previously calculated and located... There's no way this could be the wrong place.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: But...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: What are you two talking about? This is the place we were looking for, yeah?
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: According to my plan, we should be able to find an Originium vein here... My deduction can be proven correct, if and only if it exists...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: But, this place...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: ...is an Oriron mine.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: What...?
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: I've investigated and analyzed everything; these are all just ordinary Oriron deposits. There's no chance they're related to Catastrophe...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: ...Let alone any traces of 'extraterrestrial objects'. Where did we go wrong?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: But, why?! We clearly saw a bunch of trucks transporting Originium on our way here! How could this all just be Oriron?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: It doesn't take long for Enya to grasp the schemes of the man bonded to her by blood.
Indeed... We came to the wrong place.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The mineral vein here is not the 'Heart of Karlan', but rather, the 'Bones of Kjerag'...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The bones that uphold Kjerag's spine...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: ...Um, may I say something?
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: The Heart of Karlan is a pure core that validates faith. Man's eyes, ears, mouth, nose, and hands can deceive, and only the heart cannot. Such is written in the Kjeragandr.
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: But, I don't think we're being unfaithful or impious.
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: We all know how much we've done... and surely, She must know too.
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: Just like how give everything on the hunt, only to come up empty-handed and go hungry through the night.
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: But, we must have faith. Faith in the land of Kjerag. Faith in Kjeragandr's protection. She promised us that as long as we have our two hands, we shall not starve or freeze to death in Kjerag's lands.
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: So, Great Saintess, come. Let's try again––
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: Huh? Try... what?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Use your bell to call Kjeragandr.
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: That sweet bell—the sound of the Saintess who played to awaken Kjeragandr, the clear sound beloved of Her, the sound that is Her call.
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: So it says in the Kjeragandr that burdenbeasts wearing this bell will not be snatched away by wild beasts; they shall not fall ill or die young, and shall find their way back home should they ever be lost.
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: For one's 'heart to be astray' is to be lost too, right? I don't totally get what it means, but I heard it from one of the clerics at the Vine-Bead Court!
attributes.name: Joanna

attributes.content: Unlike her two dejected companions, the girl shows no signs of discouragement and is eager to try again.
That's right. We should keep trying. Perhaps Kjeragandr will respond to us.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The silver bell rings out on high, its engraved patterns shimmering beneath the hazy light of the miner's lamps.
She had tried to call out like this before. At the Vine-Bead Court, in her own bedroom, among her books, while resting among the snow-capped mountains.
Ring—ring—
The clear, cold sound of the silver bell echoes through the empty cave.
...Woah, that's...!
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: This sound, this frequency... Why does it sound so alike?!
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Did you discover something?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: That frequency! The echoes of your bell throughout the cave sound just like that one audio recording from the observatory—almost identical, even!
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Is that just a coincidence?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: A coincidence? No...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: 'In nature, no two snowflakes are exactly alike'...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Say, Saintess... Where exactly did that bell come from?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: That would require going back to very ancient legends...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: In Kjerag history, there was a long span of time where Kjeragandr fell into deep slumber until She was awoken by a devout Saintess.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: It was Kjeragandr's gift to that Saintess.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I've also heard that when subsequent craftsmen made bells for the Saintesses, they sought to imitate the sounds of the mountains...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The sounds of the mountains...? What kind of mountain could make a sound––
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: A Feranmut?
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: Impossible... How could that be...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Snap out of it, Elena... You're a scientist. How could you let your faith falter at a time like this...?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Elena, are you saying that on the day before the Catastrophe, something fell onto Mount Karlan, thus creating that sound?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Based on my previous conjectures, yes...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: So think about it this way... If we think of the mountain as a huge bell, doesn't that mean there's probably a huge hollow inside it?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Theoretically speaking, that would... be possible... But, do you know where a massive hollow like that could exist?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: There is a rumor not recorded in Kjerag's official history, and it also happens to be related to the Saintess who awakened Kjeragandr.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: A long, long time ago, there once was an uprising against the Vine-Bead Court in Kjerag.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The people had grown dissatisfied because Kjeragandr had not performed any miracles in a long time, and so they marched upon the Vine-Bead Court. Their wrath was only quelled when the Saintess awakened the sleeping deity.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: During the chaos, a number of clerics took their precious scriptures and took refuge within Mount Karlan, therefore preserving some of their valuable knowledge.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Yet regardless, the classic works of the Vine-Bead Court still suffered a break in continuity around that time.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: What if... the account of Kjeragandr leaving her people within the scriptures... is actually a description of this event?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Great Saintess... If this sanctuary truly exists, will we find traces of the 'Kjeragandr' there?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Where should we even look?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Behind the Vine-Bead Court... the place leading to Mount Karlan's summit.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Enya tightly grips the silver bell in her hand. She seems to hear the wind and the snow, as well as the footsteps that would occasionally shadow her while she worked at the Vine-Bead Court.
Whose voice was that? Who was calling to her from behind? Who would pat her on the shoulders and bring honeyed cystymilk to her while she was burning the midnight oil to organize her scriptures?
All she can hear and see is the pure white snow that never stops blowing with the wind.
O Kjeragandr on high, please grant us your guidance.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: May the path we walk be true.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: 'A cleric from the Vine-Bead Court, in collusion with Victorian agents, stole important military intelligence and was arrested yesterday.'
'Great Elder Adso confessed to some of these allegations, and agreed to fully cooperate with the investigation. During this time, all activities of the Vine-Bead Court are to be suspended.'
'There is considerable evidence suggesting that Victorian agents are involved in a string of unusual natural and social phenomena.'
'Karlan Trade is also assembling scientific researchers to assess the possibility of an espionage-related Catastrophe.'
'As a reminder to all citizens of Kjerag: If any foreign national approaches you under the guise of offering charity, business ventures, or gathering news, please be extremely vigilant...'
I knew it!
attributes.name: Angered Local

attributes.content: The Great Elder and that Victorian duke are in cahoots! Look what those bastards have brought upon us!
attributes.name: Angered Local

attributes.content: Couldn't it just be one big misunderstanding? If even the Vine-Bead Court has betrayed Kjeragandr... My goodness...
attributes.name: Anxious Local

attributes.content: They've been mucking about with the Victorians, and you still give them the benefit of the doubt?
attributes.name: Angered Local

attributes.content: But, hasn't the Karlan Trade you seem to trust so much been dealing with Victoria this whole time...?
attributes.name: Anxious Local

attributes.content: The only reason you can even hear all this on the radio is thanks to Karlan Trade. But what about the Vine-Bead Court? What've they ever done for you?
attributes.name: Angered Local

attributes.content: Stop your bickering. Victorian broadcasting has a response. Shut up and listen.
attributes.name: Nosy Local

attributes.content: I don't give a damn what they have to say!
attributes.name: Angered Local

attributes.content: ...I need to make sense of all of this.
attributes.name: Anxious Local

attributes.content: 'Sir Wilson, you are highly respected by Her Grace the Duke, as well as an expert on the Kjerag affair. We would like to ask you—how should we interpret the attitude displayed by Enciodes Silverash?
'I see it as nothing more than a way of quelling the public discontent in Kjerag. Enciodes has always been a close partner of Victoria.'
'Don't you find that somewhat difficult to believe?'
'Even based on publicly-available information, it is an indisputable fact that Victoria has been the primary beneficiary of many of the trade contracts strongly spearheaded by Enciodes himself.'

'With the help of Karlan Trade, Kjerag's development has almost entirely come off the back of Victoria's aid, from the import of breeding beasts and fertilizers to railway technology and even Originium processing.'
'In return, Enciodes has committed to handing over more than forty percent of Kjerag's natural resources for Victoria's development.'
'Not along ago, Karlan Trade also handed over a mining site that was being worked by Kazimierz to the Duke of Caster.'
'Then, it seems that Enciodes is trying to maintain an attitude of ambiguity. That being the case, are you not worried that his attempts to soothe the public will anger the Duke?'

'Lest you forget, Enciodes is the Duke's grand-nephew, and the blood of Caster flows in his veins. The delicate understanding between him and the Duke is, without a doubt, difficult for outsiders to grasp.'

'What you may not know is that even while he issued the statement, he also submitted a new mining agreement to the Duke, who has already signed and returned it. Expect a joint announcement on this matter in short time.'
'I have obtained the key points of this agreement and would like to share them with you here. Do take a look at these graphs, good sir.'
''Karlan Trade is willing to entrust 100% of future mining rights to a foundation established jointly with the Duke of Caster'. This truly is...'
'Just the tip of the iceberg. Look at what comes next...'
Nonsense!
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: The young reporter is startled by the volume of her outburst. She instinctively reaches to pat her bulging backpack before turning around.
Seeing the empty street makes her let out a heavy sigh.
Phew... Looks like they won't be able to catch up to me.
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Camera, recorder, film prints... Everything's here. This time, I won't let them take these away!
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: She then looks at the statue of Kjeragandr in the distance, pulls down the brim of her hat, and walks toward the city's center.
